edges.description: Loss of dolichol phosphate production limits downstream glycosylation reactions.
Defective dolichol-dependent glycosylation alters alpha-dystroglycan modification.
Broad glycoprotein hypoglycosylation drives multisystem manifestations.
Severe cutaneous DOLK-CDG includes epidermal lipid-handling defects that contribute to the ichthyotic skin phenotype.
Cardiac dystroglycan hypoglycosylation contributes to cardiomyopathy and heart failure.
Neuromuscular glycosylation defects contribute to generalized hypotonia.
Skin glycosylation defects contribute to ichthyosiform skin disease.
Neurologic glycosylation defects can manifest as refractory seizures.
Multisystem metabolic disease contributes to poor growth and feeding difficulty.
Abnormal glycoprotein biology can accompany chronic microcytic anemia.
Marrow and glycoprotein abnormalities can be accompanied by recurrent leukopenia.
Severe multisystem involvement can include renal dysfunction.
Severe neonatal disease may include insulin-resistant hyperglycemia.
Some patients with syndromic ichthyotic DOLK-CDG develop neonatal or infantile hypoglycemia.
Neurodevelopmental impairment is part of the broad CDG phenotype.
Severe syndromic DOLK-CDG can include microcephaly among extracutaneous manifestations.
Dysmorphic features can occur in severe multisystem disease.
Fetal hypokinesia and muscular dystrophy can contribute to limb contractures.
Defective epidermal lipid metabolism contributes to the congenital ichthyotic skin phenotype.
The severe neonatal cutaneous branch can include distal digital constriction rings.

The type I carbohydrate-deficient transferrin profile reports impaired synthesis or transfer of lipid-linked oligosaccharide precursors downstream of reduced DOLK-dependent dolichol phosphate availability.
Decreased factor XI and antithrombin III activities are laboratory readouts of abnormal glycoprotein maturation in the multisystem DK1-CDG phenotype.

nodes.description: DOLK-related glycosylation defects reduce O-mannosylation of alpha-dystroglycan in the heart, weakening extracellular-matrix linkage.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal recessive dilated cardiomyopathy due to DOLK mutations results from abnormal dystroglycan O-mannosylation.
evidence.snippet: reduced O-mannosylation of alpha-dystroglycan with concomitant functional loss of its laminin-binding capacity

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: Clotting factor XI and antithrombin III activity were decreased in all patients. Interestingly, no significant bleeding event or thrombotic event was observed in any of the patients.

readouts.interpretation: Decreased factor XI and antithrombin III activities are laboratory readouts of abnormal glycoprotein maturation in the multisystem DK1-CDG phenotype.

nodes.description: Dysmorphic facial features have been reported in severe neonatal disease.

evidence.reference_title: Severe, fatal multisystem manifestations in a patient with dolichol kinase-congenital disorder of glycosylation.
evidence.snippet: A male neonate born to non-consanguineous parents of Palestinian origin presented with dysmorphic features, genital abnormalities, talipes equinovarus, and severe, refractory generalized seizures.

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: Plasma transferrin isoelectric focusing (TIEF) is used as a simple and reliable biochemical screening tool for CDG [4].

evidence.reference_title: Severe, fatal multisystem manifestations in a patient with dolichol kinase-congenital disorder of glycosylation.
evidence.snippet: ESI-MS of transferrin (Mayo Clinic, Rochester, MN) demonstrated elevated mono-oligosaccharide:di-oligosaccharide transferrin ratio of 0.707 (reference range < 0.100) and a-oligosaccharide:di-oligosaccharide transferrin ratio of 0.216 (reference range < 0.050). These ratios are indicative of Type I CDG, resulting in impaired synthesis or transfer of the LLO precursor that subsequently generates proteins with unoccupied glycosylation sites [2].

readouts.interpretation: The type I carbohydrate-deficient transferrin profile reports impaired synthesis or transfer of lipid-linked oligosaccharide precursors downstream of reduced DOLK-dependent dolichol phosphate availability.

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: Mutation analysis in the DK1 gene revealed homozygous mutations c.1222C > G; (p.His408Asp) in all affected surviving individuals in Family I and Family II. The homozygous mutation c.912G>T (p.Trp304Cys) was detected in all affected members of Family III.

evidence.reference_title: Severe, fatal multisystem manifestations in a patient with dolichol kinase-congenital disorder of glycosylation.
evidence.snippet: next-generation sequencing demonstrated homozygous p.Q483K DOLK mutations that were confirmed in patient fibroblasts to result in severely reduced substrate binding and catalytic activity.

evidence.reference_title: DOLK / DK1-congenital disorder of glycosylation (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: DOLK | HGNC:23406 | DK1-congenital disorder of glycosylation | MONDO:0012556 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: Biallelic DOLK variants reduce dolichol kinase activity and compromise dolichol phosphate biosynthesis in the endoplasmic reticulum membrane.

evidence.reference_title: Severe, fatal multisystem manifestations in a patient with dolichol kinase-congenital disorder of glycosylation.
evidence.snippet: Dolichol kinase (DOLK) catalyzes the final step in biosynthesis of dolichol phosphate (Dol-P), which is the oligosaccharide carrier required for protein N-glycosylation.

nodes.description: Global glycoprotein hypoglycosylation contributes to neurologic, skin, hematologic, and renal manifestations across the DK1-CDG spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: CDG is generally associated with multi-organ symptoms, including psychomotor retardation, ataxia, polyneuropathy, epilepsy, endocrine abnormalities, growth retardation, visual and hearing loss, ichthyosis, cardiac, renal, liver, and gastrointestinal involvement [2, 5–9].

evidence.reference_title: Severe, fatal multisystem manifestations in a patient with dolichol kinase-congenital disorder of glycosylation.
evidence.snippet: Symptoms generally associated with CDGs include profound global delay, epilepsy, polyneuropathy, ataxia, endocrine abnormalities, ichthyosis, visual and hearing loss, cardiac, liver, renal, and gastrointestinal involvement [3].

nodes.description: Dilated cardiomyopathy is the most consistent and clinically dominant manifestation of DK1-CDG.

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: A lethal form of DCM has been described in DK1-CDG (dolichol kinase defect, MIM 610768; 9q34.11 due to mutations in TMEM15, also labeled as the DK1 and DOLK gene) [2, 17].

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: The cardiac symptoms varied from discrete, mild dilation to overt heart failure with death.

nodes.description: Distal digital constrictions are a striking feature of severe neonatal DOLK-CDG and may present as circumferential constriction rings on the digits.

evidence.reference_title: Dolichol kinase deficiency (DOLK-CDG): Two new cases and expansion of phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Both patients presented in the neonatal period with severe ichthyosis, unusual distal digital constrictions and dilated cardiomyopathy which resulted in death.

evidence.reference_title: Fatal Neonatal DOLK-CDG as a Rare Form of Syndromic Ichthyosis.
evidence.snippet: We report two neonatal cases of lethal ichthyosis from the same family, with distal digital constrictions and a progressive course leading to multi-organ failure and death.

nodes.description: DOLK-related glycosylation abnormalities can disrupt epidermal lipid metabolism, producing keratinocyte lipid-droplet accumulation and severe ichthyosis.

evidence.reference_title: Dolichol kinase deficiency (DOLK-CDG): Two new cases and expansion of phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Histology of the skin showed lipid droplet accumulation in the stratum corneum and keratinocytes, which suggests defective epidermal lipid metabolism.

nodes.description: Feeding difficulty and poor growth are reported in some affected children.

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: Patient I/6, a younger sister of patient I/5, had a history of mild hypotonia, failure to thrive, short stature, and ichthyosiform dermatitis. She was diagnosed with mild dilated cardiomyopathy (LVEDD and LVESD Z-scores 2.27 and 2.38, respectively) at the age of 6 without any clinical symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Severe, fatal multisystem manifestations in a patient with dolichol kinase-congenital disorder of glycosylation.
evidence.snippet: He had gastroesophageal reflux and dysphagia, failed to thrive, and eventually required a gastrostomy tube for enteral feeding.

nodes.description: Developmental delay is a recognized part of the broader DK1-CDG spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Severe, fatal multisystem manifestations in a patient with dolichol kinase-congenital disorder of glycosylation.
evidence.snippet: Symptoms generally associated with CDGs include profound global delay, epilepsy, polyneuropathy, ataxia, endocrine abnormalities, ichthyosis, visual and hearing loss, cardiac, liver, renal, and gastrointestinal involvement [3].

nodes.description: Cardiac transplantation can be life-saving for progressive or refractory DK1-CDG cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: Three children had repeated TIEF analysis upon cardiac transplantation.

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: They all went through successful heart transplantation;

nodes.description: Severe insulin-resistant hyperglycemia has been reported in the neonatal multisystem presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Severe, fatal multisystem manifestations in a patient with dolichol kinase-congenital disorder of glycosylation.
evidence.snippet: At age 3 months, the patient developed sinus tachycardia, hypertension, unexplained hypokalemia (K+ 3.0 mEq/L, reference range 3.6–6.0 mEq/L), and hyperglycemia (glucose 461 mg/dL, reference range 65–110 mg/dL) despite low glucose infusion rate of 2 mg/kg/minute. While potassium levels eventually stabilized, his glucose remained markedly elevated without ketoacidosis. Even high continuous insulin infusion (1.3 units/kg/hour) did not stabilize the glucose.

nodes.description: Hypoglycemia has also been reported within the fatal neonatal syndromic ichthyosis presentation of DOLK-CDG.

evidence.reference_title: Fatal Neonatal DOLK-CDG as a Rare Form of Syndromic Ichthyosis.
evidence.snippet: the congenital skin phenotype (often ichthyosis) is later associated with variable extracutaneous features such as dilatative cardiomyopathy, epilepsy, microcephaly, visual impairment, and hypoglycemia and may lead to a fatal course.

nodes.description: Ichthyosis or severe hyperkeratotic skin disease is a recurrent cutaneous phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: All patients had significant skin findings (hyperkeratosis, ichthyosis, minimal hair growth), and one child had symptoms of hypoglycemia, failure to thrive, and liver involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Fatal Neonatal DOLK-CDG as a Rare Form of Syndromic Ichthyosis.
evidence.snippet: the congenital skin phenotype (often ichthyosis) is later associated with variable extracutaneous features such as dilatative cardiomyopathy, epilepsy, microcephaly, visual impairment, and hypoglycemia and may lead to a fatal course.

nodes.description: Recurrent leukopenia has been reported alongside chronic microcytic anemia in cohort studies of DK1-CDG.

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: Most patients presented with chronic microcytic anemia and recurrent episodes of leucopenia without thrombocytopenia.

nodes.description: Microcephaly can occur in severe neonatal DOLK-CDG and expands the extracutaneous phenotype beyond cardiac and skin disease.

evidence.reference_title: Fatal Neonatal DOLK-CDG as a Rare Form of Syndromic Ichthyosis.
evidence.snippet: the congenital skin phenotype (often ichthyosis) is later associated with variable extracutaneous features such as dilatative cardiomyopathy, epilepsy, microcephaly, visual impairment, and hypoglycemia and may lead to a fatal course.

nodes.description: Chronic microcytic anemia is common in the Israeli cohort.

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: Most patients presented with chronic microcytic anemia and recurrent episodes of leucopenia without thrombocytopenia.

nodes.description: Hypotonia is a frequent early neurologic feature in DK1-CDG.

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: The neonatally observed neurological involvement and severe muscular hypotonia dominated the clinical presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Severe, fatal multisystem manifestations in a patient with dolichol kinase-congenital disorder of glycosylation.
evidence.snippet: Physical examination showed systolic heart murmur, hypotonia, bilateral talipes equinovarus, sacral dimple with hairy tuft, localized lower extremity hypertrichosis, and penoscrotal fusion.

nodes.description: Dolichol phosphate is required for N-glycosylation and for other dolichol- dependent glycosylation pathways, so DOLK deficiency causes broad glycan synthesis defects.

evidence.reference_title: Severe, fatal multisystem manifestations in a patient with dolichol kinase-congenital disorder of glycosylation.
evidence.snippet: N-linked CDG was suspected when ESI-MS of transferrin (Mayo Clinic, Rochester, MN) demonstrated elevated mono-oligosaccharide:di-oligosaccharide transferrin ratio of 0.707 (reference range < 0.100) and a-oligosaccharide:di-oligosaccharide transferrin ratio of 0.216 (reference range < 0.050). These ratios are indicative of Type I CDG, resulting in impaired synthesis or transfer of the LLO precursor that subsequently generates proteins with unoccupied glycosylation sites [2].

nodes.description: Severe DK1-CDG may progress to renal dysfunction or renal failure.

evidence.reference_title: Severe, fatal multisystem manifestations in a patient with dolichol kinase-congenital disorder of glycosylation.
evidence.snippet: He also developed renal failure and hepatic synthetic dysfunction as evidenced by electrolyte disturbances, anuria, hepatomegaly, elevated transaminases, and ascites.

nodes.description: Seizures can occur in severe neonatal and infantile DK1-CDG.

evidence.reference_title: Severe, fatal multisystem manifestations in a patient with dolichol kinase-congenital disorder of glycosylation.
evidence.snippet: The events were found to be caused by partial or generalized seizures, as an electroencephalogram revealed non-specific encephalopathy and generalized multifocal seizures.

evidence.reference_title: Dolichol kinase deficiency (DOLK-CDG) with a purely neurological presentation caused by a novel mutation.
evidence.snippet: A 4-month old boy presented with multiple epileptic seizure types including West syndrome.

nodes.description: Standard heart-failure therapy with ACE inhibitors, beta blockers, diuretics, and digoxin is used to stabilize patients with milder dilated cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: The other 4 children diagnosed with mild dilated cardiomyopathy are doing well on supportive heart failure therapy.

evidence.reference_title: From discrete dilated cardiomyopathy to successful cardiac transplantation in congenital disorders of glycosylation due to dolichol kinase deficiency (DK1-CDG).
evidence.snippet: They include ACE inhibitors and β blockers, in addition to diuretics and even Digoxin.

nodes.description: Congenital foot deformities can accompany the severe multisystem phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Severe, fatal multisystem manifestations in a patient with dolichol kinase-congenital disorder of glycosylation.
evidence.snippet: Physical examination showed systolic heart murmur, hypotonia, bilateral talipes equinovarus, sacral dimple with hairy tuft, localized lower extremity hypertrichosis, and penoscrotal fusion.
